policy_statement: Use T_K as the default independent variable for transition-shape derivative candidates
dM_dt_emu_per_s may be reported as timing diagnostic only and must not replace dM_dT for transition-shape outputs
time_s must not be treated as elapsed time and elapsed semantics require explicit time_rel_s
Compute derivatives only within file_id plus segment_id scoped windows
segment_type UNKNOWN may support basic diagnostic derivative checks only and cannot support ZFC FCC FCW interpretation
Default derivative source channel is M_emu_clean
M_emu_smooth may be used only when explicitly declared in provenance with method and smoothing parameters
Allow local central finite difference with declared window and boundary behavior
Allow local robust slope over declared T window with declared robust fit option
Minimum local window is 3 points for central difference and 5 for robust slope
Minimum points per segment derivative scope is 7
T spacing nonuniformity metrics must be reported per derivative scope
pause gaps in time_s do not alone invalidate dM_dT if T ordering and spacing gates pass
Block derivative output when T deltas are repeated near-zero beyond tolerance or too sparse for method
If T_K is nonmonotonic in scope derivative is blocked unless policy-split into monotonic subscopes is declared
Do not silently interpolate or regrid T for derivative computations
Interpolation is forbidden unless separately approved interpolation policy and provenance declaration exist
Transition outputs are candidate-only diagnostics and not physics claims
